text: ‘Came to terms with them’; literally, ‘said his say’.

It is possible to interpret the Hebrew text as implying that Jephte meant to sacrifice a beast; but in any case (as the sequel discloses) he must have included human as well as animal life in his vow. Some think he made the vow by a special inspiration; but it is perhaps the better opinion that it was a vow sinful in itself, but prompted (as an act of supreme devotion) by an erroneous conscience.
